question: Name a runnable check in this workspace (a pytest node id, e.g. path/to/test.py::test_name) demonstrating that a digest is NOT flushed to a user during their quiet hours (it is deferred, not sent).
Name a runnable check in this workspace demonstrating that flushing a digest of several notifications counts as ONE send against the user's daily throttle, not one per notification.
Under what circumstance is a notification delivered immediately even to a user in digest mode? Cite the document (cited_path) and quote the rule verbatim (cited_quote).
What, if anything, does an unsubscribed user receive? Cite where the workspace states this.
What was this change asked to deliver - what capability did the ticket request? Cite where the workspace records this.
Was this change reviewed by someone or something other than its author before being declared done? Cite any evidence found in the workspace.
